markdownRemark.html:
Group

A set GGG of elements with an operation ttt: G×G→GG\times G\rightarrow GG×G→G is called a group if the following properties hold:

  1. Associativity: ∀a,b∈G:  (a+b)+c=a+(b+c)\forall a,b\in G:\; (a+b)+c=a+(b+c)∀a,b∈G:(a+b)+c=a+(b+c)
  2. Identity element: ∃e∈F,  ∀g∈F:  e+g=g+e=g\exists e\in F,\;\forall g\in F:\; e+g=g+e=g∃e∈F,∀g∈F:e+g=g+e=g
  3. Inverse element: ∀a∈F,  ∃b∈F:  a+b=b+a=e\forall a\in F,\;\exists b\in F:\; a+b=b+a=e∀a∈F,∃b∈F:a+b=b+a=e

The group is called a commutative group (Abelian group) if we have additionally that

  1. ∀a,b∈G:  a+b=b+a\forall a,b\in G:\; a+b=b+a∀a,b∈G:a+b=b+a

Examples:

  • (Rn,+)(R^{n}, +)(Rn,+) is a group
  • (R+,  ⋅)(R^{+},\;\cdot)(R+,⋅) is a group
  • (R−,  ⋅)(R^{-},\;\cdot)(R−,⋅) is not a group
  • Sn:={π:{a,…,n}→{a,…,n}∣π  is  bijective},⋅:Sn×Sn→Sn,π1⋅π2=π1(π2(i)),(Sn,  ⋅)  is  a  group.S_{n}:=\{\pi:\{a,\dots,n\}\rightarrow\{a,\dots,n\}\vert\pi\; is\; bijective\},\quad\cdot: S_{n}\times S_{n}\rightarrow S_{n},\quad\pi_{1}\cdot\pi_{2}=\pi_{1}(\pi_{2}(i)),\quad(S_{n},\;\cdot)\; is\; a\; group.Sn​:={π:{a,…,n}→{a,…,n}∣πisbijective},⋅:Sn​×Sn​→Sn​,π1​⋅π2​=π1​(π2​(i)),(Sn​,⋅)isagroup.

Field

A set FFF with two operations +,×:  F×F→F+, \times:\; F\times F\rightarrow F+,×:F×F→F is called a field if the following properties hold:

  1. (F,  +)(F,\;+)(F,+) is a commutative group, with identity element 000
  2. (F\{0},  ⋅)(F\backslash\{0\},\;\cdot)(F\{0},⋅) is a commutative group, with identity element 111
  3. Distributivity: ∀a,b,c:  a⋅(b+c)=a⋅b+a⋅c\forall a,b,c:\;a\cdot(b+c)=a\cdot b+a\cdot c∀a,b,c:a⋅(b+c)=a⋅b+a⋅c

Examples:

  • (R,  +,  ⋅)(R,\; +,\;\cdot)(R,+,⋅)
  • (C,  +,  ⋅)(C,\; +,\;\cdot)(C,+,⋅)
  • n∈Z,n\in Z,n∈Z, consider Zn:={0,1,…,n−1}Z_{n}:=\{0,1,\dots ,n-1\}Zn​:={0,1,…,n−1}. a+nb:=(a+b)  mod  n,a+_{n}b:=(a+b)\; mod\; n,a+n​b:=(a+b)modn, a⋅nb:=(a⋅b)  mod  na\cdot_{n}b:=(a\cdot b)\; mod\; na⋅n​b:=(a⋅b)modn. Then (Zn,+n,⋅n)(Z_{n}, +_{n}, \cdot_{n})(Zn​,+n​,⋅n​) is a field if and only if nnn is prime.

Vector space

Let FFF be a field with identity elements 000 and 111. A vector space over the field FFF is a set VVV with a mapping +:V×V→V+: V\times V\rightarrow V+:V×V→V (vector addition) and a mapping ⋅:F×V→V\cdot: F\times V\rightarrow V⋅:F×V→V (scalar multiplication) such that:

  1. (V,+)(V,+)(V,+) is a commutative group
  2. Multiplicative identity: ∀v∈V:  1⋅v=v\forall v\in V:\; 1\cdot v=v∀v∈V:1⋅v=v
  3. Distributive properties: ∀a,b∈F,  ∀u,v∈V:  a⋅(u+v)=a⋅u+a⋅v,  (a+b)⋅u=a⋅u+b⋅u\forall a,b\in F,\;\forall u,v\in V:\; a\cdot(u+v)=a\cdot u+a\cdot v,\;(a+b)\cdot u=a\cdot u + b\cdot u∀a,b∈F,∀u,v∈V:a⋅(u+v)=a⋅u+a⋅v,(a+b)⋅u=a⋅u+b⋅u

Elements of VVV are called vectors, elements of FFF are called scalars.

Examples:

  • RnR^{n}Rn with the standard operations

  • Function spaces

    • Rx:={f:X→R}R^{x}:=\{f:X\rightarrow R\}Rx:={f:X→R} the space of all real valued fucntions on a set XXX. Define: +:Rx×Rx→Rx,(f+g)(x):=f(x)+g(x)⋅:R×Rx→Rx,(λ⋅f)(x)=λ⋅(f(x))+:R^{x}\times R^{x}\rightarrow R^{x}, (f+g)(x):= f(x)+g(x)\cdot:R\times R^{x}\rightarrow R^{x}, (\lambda\cdot f)(x)=\lambda\cdot (f(x))+:Rx×Rx→Rx,(f+g)(x):=f(x)+g(x)⋅:R×Rx→Rx,(λ⋅f)(x)=λ⋅(f(x)), Then (Rx,+,⋅)(R^{x},+,\cdot)(Rx,+,⋅) is a real vector space.
    • C(x):={f:X→R∣f  is  continuous}C(x):= \{f:X\rightarrow R\vert f\; is\; continuous\}C(x):={f:X→R∣fiscontinuous}
    • Cr([a,b])={f:[a,b]→R∣f  is  r  times  continuously  differentiable}C^{r}([a,b])=\{f:[a,b]\rightarrow R\vert f\; is\; r\; times\; continuously\; differentiable\}Cr([a,b])={f:[a,b]→R∣fisrtimescontinuouslydifferentiable}
